title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Broken-Build Bay
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a dead-code and dependency audit and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.
Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Unused Symbols trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.

content.text: For Unused Symbols, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Unused Symbols precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Prefixing every unused value with an underscore fixes dead code.

stimulus.title: Unused Symbols case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.text: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics. Artifact under review: a dead-code and dependency audit.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Unused Symbols, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-safe-removal-7ig25n: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Removal into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-safe-removal-7ig25n:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Safe Removal applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
